title: Opening Store Checklist
steps: Arrive 30 minutes before store opening.
Disable the alarm using the keypad (code in manager's binder).
Turn on all lights aisle by aisle using the panel in the back office.
Check the overnight freight log and sign off.
Walk each aisle for safety hazards (spills, fallen items, blocked exits).
Verify POS terminals boot correctly — reboot any frozen terminals.
Confirm all refrigerated sections are at correct temp (dairy: 35–38°F, frozen: 0°F or below).
Unlock customer-entry doors at opening time.
Announce store opening over intercom: 'Good morning, shoppers. Welcome to our store!'

title: Closing Store Checklist
steps: Announce closing 15 minutes before close: 'Attention shoppers, the store will close in 15 minutes.'
Begin escorting remaining customers to checkout.
Lock all entrance doors once last customer has exited.
Complete end-of-day cash reconciliation at each POS lane.
Process Z-tape from all registers and deposit cash in the safe.
Secure all high-value merchandise (electronics, pharmacy) in lockable cabinets.
Check restrooms for remaining customers and safety.
Turn off non-essential lights; leave safety/emergency lighting active.
Enable alarm and lock back office.
Complete closing log with your employee ID and timestamp.

title: Receiving & Stocking Freight
steps: Check delivery manifest against purchase order before accepting shipment.
Inspect pallets for damage; note discrepancies on the delivery receipt.
Scan each carton barcode into the inventory management system (IMS).
Move freight to the appropriate staging area by department.
Stock items using FIFO (first-in, first-out): pull existing stock forward, place new stock behind.
Verify price stickers/shelf tags match the IMS price before shelving.
Crush and bale all cardboard in the compactor.
Update IMS once all items are stocked.
Report any discrepancies to the department manager within 2 hours.

title: Handling Customer Returns
steps: Greet the customer and ask for their receipt or order confirmation.
Check the return policy: most items accepted within 90 days with receipt.
Inspect the item for damage and ensure original packaging where required.
Process return on POS: select 'Return' > scan item barcode > confirm amount.
Issue refund to original payment method or store credit if no receipt.
For perishables, dispose of returned item; do NOT restock food returns.
For electronics: follow the Electronics Return SOP (SOP009).
Log the return reason for shrink tracking.
Thank the customer and offer assistance with finding a replacement.

title: Price Check & Override Procedure
steps: If a customer disputes a price, scan the item at any POS terminal.
Compare displayed price with shelf tag.
If shelf tag price is lower, honor the shelf tag price (company policy).
To override: press 'Price Override' on POS, enter manager's override code.
Print a price correction form and attach to the shelf tag for the pricing team.
Log the price discrepancy in the Price Accuracy log.
Thank the customer for bringing it to our attention.

title: Spill & Hazard Cleanup
steps: Immediately block off the spill area using wet floor signs.
Radio/call for cleanup support: 'Cleanup needed in Aisle [X].'
For liquid spills: apply absorbent granules, sweep, mop with cleaning solution.
For broken glass: use dustpan and broom, never hands; dispose in sharps/glass bin.
Inspect area to ensure floor is dry before removing wet floor signs.
Complete a Safety Incident Report if any customer was involved.
Restock any damaged product or remove it from sale.

title: Shelf Replenishment & Facing
steps: Pull stock from backroom using the replenishment report printed each morning.
Use a stocking cart; never leave stock unattended in the aisle.
Follow planogram layouts — check the section planogram card for correct placement.
Apply FIFO: new stock behind existing stock.
Face all items: fronts forward, labels readable by the customer.
Consolidate slow-moving items to one shelf position; notify manager if shelf is overstocked.
Check for expired items; pull and quarantine all expired stock.
Update the facing count in the IMS when done.

title: Loss Prevention & Shoplifting Protocol
steps: Greet all customers in high-theft areas (electronics, health & beauty) — visibility is deterrence.
If you observe suspected shoplifting, do NOT confront the individual.
Radio Loss Prevention team with description: 'LP, I have a code 10 in Aisle [X].'
Maintain visual contact if safe to do so; note direction of travel.
LP staff will handle apprehension according to state law.
Complete an Incident Report with date, time, description, and any footage timestamps.
Never put yourself or customers in danger.

title: Electronics Return & Exchange
steps: Inspect returned electronics for physical damage and completeness of contents.
Open box and verify all accessories, cables, and documentation are present.
Power-on test: confirm the device boots and there are no obvious defects.
For personal data concerns (phones/tablets), wipe device using factory reset before restocking.
Open-box items: re-seal and apply 'Open Box — [discount]%' label.
Complete exchange if same item is requested; else process as return.
Log the IMEI/serial number in the Electronics Return Log.

title: Cold Chain Compliance — Refrigerated & Frozen
steps: Check refrigerator and freezer temperatures at opening and closing using the temperature log.
Acceptable ranges: refrigerated = 35–40°F; frozen = -10°F to 0°F.
If a cooler exceeds threshold, notify the store manager and maintenance immediately.
Do NOT stock new product into a malfunctioning cooler.
Move at-risk product to a working unit or quarantine for disposal.
Products above 45°F for more than 2 hours must be discarded (food safety).
Document all temperature events and corrective actions in the Cold Chain Log.

title: Customer Complaint Escalation
steps: Listen to the customer's complaint without interrupting.
Apologize and thank the customer for bringing the issue to your attention.
Attempt to resolve at floor level: replacement, refund, or alternative product.
If unable to resolve, call the department manager to assist.
Never argue with a customer; de-escalate with calm language.
Document the complaint in the Customer Feedback Log.
If the complaint involves a food safety issue, escalate to store manager immediately.

title: Planogram Reset Procedure
steps: Print new planogram from the Merchandising portal.
Clear the section to be reset, placing items on a staging cart.
Clean shelves with all-purpose cleaner before placing new product.
Set shelf height according to planogram spec.
Place items in planogram-specified positions — verify SKU and facing count.
Apply or update shelf price tags to match IMS prices.
Take a completion photo and upload to the Merchandising portal.
Notify the store manager once the reset is complete.

title: Expired & Damaged Product Removal
steps: Perform daily date checks on dairy, bakery, meat, and deli items.
Perform weekly date checks on canned, packaged, and frozen goods.
Pull any product at or past its best-by/use-by date.
Place pulled items in the 'Quarantine Cart' in the back room.
Complete the Out-of-Date Log with SKU, product name, qty, and date.
Damaged goods with intact safety seals may be discounted; consult manager.
Dispose of food products per the store's food waste policy.

title: Register Open & Cash Handling
steps: Collect your assigned cash drawer from the safe with witness present.
Count the opening float ($200 standard) and sign the Cash Count Sheet.
Verify register paper roll is loaded and POS terminal is online.
Do not keep more than $300 in your drawer; perform cash drops as needed.
Accept only non-mutilated bills; use counterfeit pen on $50 and $100 bills.
At shift end, count drawer with a supervisor; complete the Closing Cash Count Sheet.
Never leave your drawer open and unattended.

title: Promotional Signage & Offer Setup
steps: Download the weekly ad from the Merchandising portal every Monday morning.
Print and cut promotional shelf talkers and price signs.
Place signs at the correct shelf position next to the promoted SKU.
Update the POS system to reflect promotional prices (use Promotion Manager module).
Verify scanned price matches advertised price for first 5 transactions of any new promo.
Remove expired signage every Sunday evening before new ad week starts.
Report any sign/price mismatches to the pricing coordinator immediately.
